column2: Your tea is on the table.

column1: Your breakfast is delicious.

column1: Unfortunately, you never really cared for sauerkraut and knockwurst, but you are too polite to tell Shema that.  You must admit the honey and lemons on the knockwurst aren't too bad, but the sauerkraut curry is a bit much.

column2: I fear, Hero-friend of the Katta, that Shapeir again needs your aid.  There is talk that an Elemental of the Earth is destroying the city.

column2: The poet will soon speak, if you will but be seated.

column2: It is said that Uhura at the Guild Hall has a message for you as well.

column1: Did you have a chance to meet and speak with those in our fair bazaar?  It is said there are many rumors in this city (but don't quote me on that).
column2: Did you have a chance to meet and speak with those in our fair bazaar?  It is said there are many rumors in this city (but don't quote me on that).

column2: It is a good thing that you are around to save us, %s.

column2: I am here to serve you, Hero.

column2: We are most honored by your presence at our Inn, Hero of Spielburg.

column1: You awaken much refreshed.
column2: You awaken much refreshed.

column1: I am so sorry that I have no answer to your question.
column2: I am so sorry that I have no answer to your question.

column1: Forgive me, Hero-friend, but I am ignorant of such things.
column2: Forgive me, Hero-friend, but I am ignorant of such things.

column1: It is good to be here again.  You will always be welcome in our Inn for the help you have given that allowed us to return here.
column2: It is good to be here again.  You will always be welcome in our Inn for the help you have given that allowed us to return here.

column1: All Kattas in that city were driven out of their homes there.  Only my cousin, Sharaf, did not leave.  I fear for him.
column2: All Kattas in that city were driven out of their homes there.  Only my cousin, Sharaf, did not leave.  I fear for him.

column1: I am not as skilled in the ways of magic as some.  I know only the quiet magic of the dance, and of how to make an inn seem a home.
column2: I am not as skilled in the ways of magic as some.  I know only the quiet magic of the dance, and of how to make an inn seem a home.

column1: The Sultan here has always protected and welcomed our people.
column2: The Sultan here has always protected and welcomed our people.

column1: The old Emir of Raseir was a kind and gentle man.  I cannot understand how the new Emir can be so cruel to our people.
column2: The old Emir of Raseir was a kind and gentle man.  I cannot understand how the new Emir can be so cruel to our people.

column1: The Palace of the Sultan is east of our Inn.  You can see it over the gates from the Plaza of the Palace.
column2: The Palace of the Sultan is east of our Inn.  You can see it over the gates from the Plaza of the Palace.

column1: The journey to Raseir is very far and very dangerous.  It can only be made by caravan.  If you do journey there, please look for my cousin, Sharaf.
column2: The journey to Raseir is very far and very dangerous.  It can only be made by caravan.  If you do journey there, please look for my cousin, Sharaf.

column1: There are many dangers in the desert.  You must be very careful there.
column2: There are many dangers in the desert.  You must be very careful there.

column1: You have no need of money in our home.  You will always be our guest.
column2: You have no need of money in our home.  You will always be our guest.

column2: The Adventurer's Guild is northwest, off the Plaza of the Fighters.
